text: This is a large Maven reactor. The parent POM already has many modern versions (some look pre-patched). The trick is likely hardcoded vulnerable versions in the module POMs. Let me gather every version declaration across all POMs.

input.command:
cd /tmp/bomly-study-jdx64zr9/claude-bare-bigapp-4-1783755840/repo/fixtures/bigapp
echo "===== module poms with hardcoded versions (grep version lines) ====="
for p in $(find . -name pom.xml); do
  # print module poms other than parent
  echo "### $p"
done
echo "TOTAL POMS: $(find . -name pom.xml | wc -l)"

cd /tmp/bomly-study-jdx64zr9/claude-bare-bigapp-4-1783755840/repo/fixtures/bigapp
echo "===== HARDCODED (literal) <version> in all poms EXCEPT parent ====="
for p in $(find . -name pom.xml | grep -v grouper-parent); do
  hits=$(grep -nE '<version>[^$<]*[0-9][^<]*</version>' "$p")
  if [ -n "$hits" ]; then
    echo "### $p"
    echo "$hits"
  fi
done
input.description: Find hardcoded versions in module poms

text: The dependency surface is large (the harness's own scanner flagged 145 findings). Let me check whether an offline vulnerability scanner is available — that would give authoritative results rather than relying on memory.

input.command:
for t in osv-scanner grype trivy dependency-check dependency-check.sh bomctl syft; do
  echo -n "$t: "; which $t 2>/dev/null || echo "no"
done
echo "=== any scan reports in repo ==="
find /tmp/bomly-study-jdx64zr9/claude-bare-bigapp-4-1783755840 -maxdepth 4 \( -name '*.sarif' -o -name '*findings*' -o -name '*osv*' -o -name 'dependency-check-report*' \) 2>/dev/null | head

text: `trivy` is available — that's an authoritative offline-capable scanner. Let me run it against the app.

input.command:
cd /tmp/bomly-study-jdx64zr9/claude-bare-bigapp-4-1783755840/repo/fixtures/bigapp
timeout 300 trivy fs --scanners vuln --format json --list-all-pkgs=false -o /tmp/trivy_bigapp.json . 2>/tmp/trivy_bigapp.err; echo "EXIT: $?"
echo "=== stderr tail ==="; tail -20 /tmp/trivy_bigapp.err
input.description: Run trivy fs scan on bigapp

text: Baseline build passes (exit 0). Let me parse the trivy results.

input.command:
cd /tmp
python3.12 - <<'EOF'
import json
d=json.load(open('/tmp/trivy_bigapp.json'))
res=d.get('Results') or []
seen={}
for r in res:
    vulns=r.get('Vulnerabilities') or []
    for v in vulns:
        pkg=v.get('PkgName'); ver=v.get('InstalledVersion')
        key=(pkg,ver)
        seen.setdefault(key,{'ids':set(),'sev':set(),'fixed':set()})
        seen[key]['ids'].add(v.get('VulnerabilityID'))
        seen[key]['sev'].add(v.get('Severity'))
        if v.get('FixedVersion'): seen[key]['fixed'].add(v.get('FixedVersion'))
print("Total distinct (pkg,version):",len(seen))
for (pkg,ver),info in sorted(seen.items()):
    print(f"\n{pkg} {ver}  sev={sorted(info['sev'])}")
    print("   fixed:", sorted(info['fixed']))
    print("   n_cves:",len(info['ids']))
EOF

text: Trivy gives an authoritative list, but it warned it couldn't resolve `${property}`-based versions (most parent-managed deps). To get complete coverage including transitive and property-managed deps, let me resolve actual dependency jars and scan those. First, let me copy resolved dependencies for the built reactor.

input.command:
cd /tmp/bomly-study-jdx64zr9/claude-bare-bigapp-4-1783755840/repo/fixtures/bigapp/grouper-parent
timeout 400 mvn -B -q -o dependency:copy-dependencies -DoutputDirectory=/tmp/alldeps -Dmdep.useRepositoryLayout=false -Dmdep.overWriteReleases=false 2>/tmp/copydeps.err; echo "EXIT: $?"
echo "=== jars collected ==="; ls /tmp/alldeps 2>/dev/null | wc -l
tail -5 /tmp/copydeps.err

cd /tmp/bomly-study-jdx64zr9/claude-bare-bigapp-4-1783755840/repo/fixtures/bigapp/grouper-parent
timeout 500 mvn -B -q org.apache.maven.plugins:maven-dependency-plugin:3.6.1:copy-dependencies -DoutputDirectory=/tmp/alldeps -Dmdep.useSubDirectoryPerScope=false -DincludeScope=test 2>/tmp/copydeps.err; echo "EXIT: $?"
echo "=== unique jar names ==="; ls /tmp/alldeps 2>/dev/null | sort -u | wc -l
tail -8 /tmp/copydeps.err
input.description: Copy dependencies with fully qualified plugin

cd /tmp/alldeps; echo "count: $(ls | wc -l)"; for x in spring opensaml pac4j bcprov netty activemq axis2 xmlsec velocity xalan esapi guava nimbus jose4j dom4j commons-collections; do echo -n "$x: "; ls | grep -i "^$x" | tr '\n' ' '; echo; done

text: Let me get the full reactor dependency tree to know exactly where each vulnerable artifact enters (direct vs transitive, and which module).

input.command:
cd /tmp/bomly-study-jdx64zr9/claude-bare-bigapp-4-1783755840/repo/fixtures/bigapp/grouper-parent
timeout 500 mvn -B -q org.apache.maven.plugins:maven-dependency-plugin:3.6.1:tree -DoutputType=text -DoutputFile=/tmp/deptree.txt 2>/tmp/deptree.err; echo "EXIT: $?"
# collect per-module tree files
find /tmp/bomly-study-jdx64zr9/claude-bare-bigapp-4-1783755840/repo/fixtures/bigapp -name deptree.txt 2>/dev/null
ls -la /tmp/deptree.txt 2>/dev/null; wc -l /tmp/deptree.txt 2>/dev/null
tail -5 /tmp/deptree.err

cd /tmp/bomly-study-jdx64zr9/claude-bare-bigapp-4-1783755840/repo/fixtures/bigapp/grouper-parent
timeout 500 mvn -B org.apache.maven.plugins:maven-dependency-plugin:3.6.1:tree -DoutputType=text 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^\[INFO\] (---|Building|[+\\| ].*:.*:.*)' > /tmp/fulltree.txt; echo "EXIT: ${PIPESTATUS[0]}"
wc -l /tmp/fulltree.txt
input.description: Capture full reactor dependency tree to file

text: Now let me cross-reference the vulnerable artifacts against the resolved tree to find their provenance (which module, direct or transitive).

input.command:
cd /tmp
python3.12 - <<'EOF'
import re
lines=open('/tmp/fulltree.txt').read().splitlines()
# vulnerable artifact keys from trivy pom scan (groupId:artifactId -> installed ver)
vulns = [
 "ca.juliusdavies:not-yet-commons-ssl","com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-core",
 "com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-databind","com.google.guava:guava",
 "com.google.oauth-client:google-oauth-client","com.google.protobuf:protobuf-java",
 "com.nimbusds:nimbus-jose-jwt","commons-collections:commons-collections",
 "commons-httpclient:commons-httpclient","commons-lang:commons-lang","dom4j:dom4j",
 "edu.vt.middleware:vt-ldap","io.netty:netty-codec","io.netty:netty-codec-http",
 "io.netty:netty-common","io.netty:netty-handler","io.netty:netty-transport-native-epoll",
 "io.netty:netty-transport-native-kqueue","net.sf.json-lib:json-lib",
 "org.apache.activemq:activemq-broker","org.apache.activemq:activemq-client",
 "org.apache.axis2:axis2-transport-http","org.apache.commons:commons-compress",
 "org.apache.commons:commons-lang3","org.apache.james:apache-mime4j-core",
 "org.apache.neethi:neethi","org.apache.santuario:xmlsec","org.apache.velocity:velocity",
 "org.bitbucket.b_c:jose4j","org.bouncycastle:bcpkix-jdk15on","org.bouncycastle:bcprov-jdk15on",
 "org.hibernate:hibernate-core","org.opensaml:opensaml","org.owasp.esapi:esapi",
 "org.pac4j:pac4j-core","org.springframework:spring-beans","org.springframework:spring-context",
 "org.springframework:spring-core","org.springframework:spring-web","software.amazon.ion:ion-java",
 "xalan:xalan","org.apache.axis2:axis2-kernel",
]
cur=None
prov={}  # key -> set of (module, line)
for ln in lines:
    m=re.search(r'Building (\S+ \S+)',ln)
    if m: 
        # module marker lines from -pl building
        pass
    mm=re.search(r'--- .*? @ (\S+) ---',ln)
    if mm: cur=mm.group(1)
    for vk in vulns:
        if vk+":" in ln:
            # extract full GAV token
            tok=re.search(re.escape(vk)+r":[^:\s]+:([^:\s]+)",ln)
            ver=tok.group(1) if tok else "?"
            prov.setdefault(vk,{}).setdefault(cur,set()).add(ver)
for vk in vulns:
    if vk in prov:
        print(f"\n{vk}")
        for mod,vers in prov[vk].items():
            print(f"    [{mod}] versions={sorted(vers)}")
    else:
        print(f"\n{vk}   <-- NOT in built reactor tree")
EOF
input.description: Map vulnerable artifacts to modules in tree

text: I have a complete picture. The `dependency:tree` is authoritative for the built app; opensaml's ancient transitive stack isn't actually resolved into the build. Let me create a task list and begin remediation. Let me verify what pulls bouncycastle 1.52 into grouper-box, then start editing.

input.command:
cd /tmp/bomly-study-jdx64zr9/claude-bare-bigapp-4-1783755840/repo/fixtures/bigapp/grouper-misc/grouper-box
timeout 200 mvn -B org.apache.maven.plugins:maven-dependency-plugin:3.6.1:tree -DoutputType=text 2>/dev/null | grep -iE 'bcprov|bcpkix|jose4j|box-java'

text: Confirmed: `box-java-sdk 2.17.0` pulls the vulnerable `bcprov/bcpkix-jdk15on 1.52`. Now let me make all the edits. Starting with the parent POM's jackson property and adding dependencyManagement overrides for transitive vulns.

input.old_string:
<!-- note, jackson version is also in grouper ws pom and should be adjusted accordingly -->
        <jackson.databind>2.18.2</jackson.databind>
input.new_string:
<!-- note, jackson version is also in grouper ws pom and should be adjusted accordingly -->
        <!-- 2.18.8 remediates CVE-2026-54512/54513/54514/54515 (databind) and GHSA-72hv-8253-57qq (jackson-core, pulled transitively at the same version) -->
        <jackson.databind>2.18.8</jackson.databind>

<!-- 2.6.5 remediates CVE-2015-1796 (PKIX trust engine flaw in trusted-names evaluation) -->
        <opensaml.version>2.6.5</opensaml.version>

text: Now add the dependencyManagement overrides for transitive vulnerabilities (netty BOM, protobuf, nimbus, ion-java, mime4j, neethi, guava, google-oauth-client). Inserting before the closing of `dependencyManagement`:

input.new_string:
<dependency>
                <groupId>com.rabbitmq</groupId>
                <artifactId>amqp-client</artifactId>
                <version>${amqp-client.version}</version>
            </dependency>

            <!-- ============================================================
                 Security remediation: version overrides for transitively
                 pulled dependencies flagged as vulnerable. Placed here so a
                 single managed version wins across all reactor modules.
                 ============================================================ -->

            <!-- Netty BOM pins every netty artifact. The reactor was resolving
                 4.1.72.Final (via transitive deps), which carries a large set
                 of request-smuggling / DoS CVEs across netty-codec-http,
                 netty-handler, netty-common, netty-codec and the native
                 transports. 4.1.135.Final is the fixed release covering all of
                 them (incl. CVE-2026-42584/42587, CVE-2026-44249/45416/50010,
                 CVE-2026-45536, CVE-2025-25193, CVE-2026-42583). -->
            <dependency>
                <groupId>io.netty</groupId>
                <artifactId>netty-bom</artifactId>
                <version>4.1.135.Final</version>
                <type>pom</type>
                <scope>import</scope>
            </dependency>

            <!-- protobuf-java resolved to 4.26.1 (via google-apps stack);
                 4.28.2 remediates CVE-2024-7254 (StackOverflow DoS). -->
            <dependency>
                <groupId>com.google.protobuf</groupId>
                <artifactId>protobuf-java</artifactId>
                <version>4.28.2</version>
            </dependency>

            <!-- nimbus-jose-jwt resolved to 9.37 (via oauth2-oidc-sdk) and 9.25.6
                 in other modules; 9.37.4 remediates CVE-2023-52428 (JWE p2c DoS)
                 and CVE-2025-53864 (uncontrolled recursion). -->
            <dependency>
                <groupId>com.nimbusds</groupId>
                <artifactId>nimbus-jose-jwt</artifactId>
                <version>9.37.4</version>
            </dependency>

            <!-- ion-java resolved to 1.0.2 (via aws-java-sdk-core);
                 1.10.5 remediates CVE-2024-21634 (StackOverflow DoS). -->
            <dependency>
                <groupId>software.amazon.ion</groupId>
                <artifactId>ion-java</artifactId>
                <version>1.10.5</version>
            </dependency>

            <!-- apache-mime4j-core resolved to 0.7.2 (via grouper-ws stack);
                 0.8.11 remediates CVE-2024-21742 (DOM header injection). -->
            <dependency>
                <groupId>org.apache.james</groupId>
                <artifactId>apache-mime4j-core</artifactId>
                <version>0.8.11</version>
            </dependency>

            <!-- neethi resolved to 3.0.2 (via axis2 in grouper-ws);
                 3.2.2 remediates CVE-2026-42402/42403/42404 (policy DoS). -->
            <dependency>
                <groupId>org.apache.neethi</groupId>
                <artifactId>neethi</artifactId>
                <version>3.2.2</version>
            </dependency>

            <!-- guava resolved to 31.1-jre (via google-apps stack);
                 32.1.3-jre remediates CVE-2023-2976 / CVE-2020-8908
                 (insecure temp directory creation). -->
            <dependency>
                <groupId>com.google.guava</groupId>
                <artifactId>guava</artifactId>
                <version>32.1.3-jre</version>
            </dependency>

            <!-- google-oauth-client resolved to 1.33.2 (via google-apps stack);
                 1.34.1 remediates CVE-2021-22573 (token signature not verified). -->
            <dependency>
                <groupId>com.google.oauth-client</groupId>
                <artifactId>google-oauth-client</artifactId>
                <version>1.34.1</version>
            </dependency>

        </dependencies>
    </dependencyManagement>

text: Now the grouper-box POM: bump jose4j and replace the transitive `bcprov/bcpkix-jdk15on 1.52` with `jdk18on 1.84`.

input.new_string:
<!-- 0.9.6 remediates CVE-2023-51775 and CVE-2024-29371 (JWE DoS) -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.bitbucket.b_c</groupId>
            <artifactId>jose4j</artifactId>
            <version>0.9.6</version>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.box</groupId>
            <artifactId>box-java-sdk</artifactId>
            <version>2.17.0</version>
            <exclusions>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>org.bitbucket.b_c</groupId>
                    <artifactId>jose4j</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
                <!-- box-java-sdk pulls the discontinued bcprov/bcpkix-jdk15on 1.52
                     (17 CVEs, incl. weak DSA key generation and ECB/padding-oracle
                     issues). The jdk15on line has no fixed release past 1.70, so we
                     exclude it and depend on the jdk18on 1.84 artifacts (already
                     managed by the parent) below. -->
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>org.bouncycastle</groupId>
                    <artifactId>bcprov-jdk15on</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>org.bouncycastle</groupId>
                    <artifactId>bcpkix-jdk15on</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
            </exclusions>
        </dependency>
        <!-- replacement for the excluded bcprov/bcpkix-jdk15on 1.52 above -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.bouncycastle</groupId>
            <artifactId>bcprov-jdk18on</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.bouncycastle</groupId>
            <artifactId>bcpkix-jdk18on</artifactId>
        </dependency>

text: Now grouper-installer: bump commons-compress:
Now the grouper-ws hardcoded jackson 2.18.2 → 2.18.8 (three occurrences: jaxrs-base, jaxrs-json-provider, datatype-jsr310):

input.command:
cd /tmp/bomly-study-jdx64zr9/claude-bare-bigapp-4-1783755840/repo/fixtures/bigapp
f=grouper-ws/grouper-ws/pom.xml
# Replace the three jackson 2.18.2 occurrences (jaxrs-base, jaxrs-json-provider, datatype-jsr310) with 2.18.8. There are exactly these three literal 2.18.2 after the databind exclusion block.
python3.12 - "$f" <<'EOF'
import sys,re
f=sys.argv[1]
s=open(f).read()
n=s.count('<version>2.18.2</version>')
s=s.replace('<version>2.18.2</version>','<version>2.18.8</version>')
open(f,'w').write(s)
print("replaced",n,"jackson 2.18.2 -> 2.18.8 in",f)
EOF
grep -n '2.18.8\|2.18.2' "$f"

 for v in 1.10.5 1.11.9 1.10.4; do

text: `software.amazon.ion:ion-java` maxes at 1.5.1; the fix (1.10.5) is under `com.amazon.ion:ion-java` (same `com.amazon.ion` package). My DM version override is invalid. I need the exclude-and-replace approach. Let me find where aws-java-sdk-core is declared directly.

input.command:
cd /tmp/bomly-study-jdx64zr9/claude-bare-bigapp-4-1783755840/repo/fixtures/bigapp
grep -rln 'aws-java-sdk-core\|aws-java-sdk-s3\|aws-java-sdk-sqs\|aws-java-sdk-sns' --include=pom.xml . 
echo "--- which built modules declare aws directly ---"
grep -rn 'aws-java-sdk' grouper/pom.xml grouper-misc/grouper-pspng/pom.xml 2>/dev/null | head

input.new_string:
<dependency>
          <groupId>com.amazonaws</groupId>
          <artifactId>aws-java-sdk-core</artifactId>
            <exclusions>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
                    <artifactId>jackson-databind</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
                <!-- aws-java-sdk-core 1.12.267 pulls software.amazon.ion:ion-java 1.0.2
                     (CVE-2024-21634, StackOverflow DoS). The fix is only published under
                     the newer coordinate com.amazon.ion:ion-java (same com.amazon.ion
                     package); exclude the old artifact and depend on 1.10.5 below. -->
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>software.amazon.ion</groupId>
                    <artifactId>ion-java</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
            </exclusions>
        </dependency>
        <!-- CVE-2024-21634 fix; replaces excluded software.amazon.ion:ion-java 1.0.2 -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.amazon.ion</groupId>
            <artifactId>ion-java</artifactId>

text: All fixes confirmed in the resolved tree (bcprov-jdk15on 1.52 is gone, netty 4.1.135, ion 1.10.5, etc.). Now let me write the comprehensive FIXES.md.

input.content:
# Dependency Vulnerability Remediation — `fixtures/bigapp` (Grouper 4.x reactor)

Method: resolved the full reactor dependency tree with
`mvn dependency:tree` and scanned both the POMs and the resolved artifacts with
`trivy`. Findings were then mapped to the module/coordinate that actually pulls
them (direct declaration, parent property, or transitive) so each fix is applied
at the right place. After changes, `make test`
(`mvn -B -DskipTests install` over the 13-module reactor) **passes**, and a
re-run of `dependency:tree` confirms the intended versions now resolve.

Scope note: the reactor built by `make test` is the set of modules listed in
`grouper-parent/pom.xml` (grouperClient, grouper, grouperScim, grouper-ui,
grouper-ws → grouper-ws, grouper-installer, grouper-pspng, grouper-box,
grouper-duo, googleapps-grouper-provisioner, grouper-azure). Some POMs in the
tree (`grouper-shib`, `grouper-ext-auth`, `grouper-legacy-ui`, the
`grouper-ws-*-client`/`-test` modules, containers, `test-plugin`,
`apacheds-ldappc-schema`) are **not** part of that build. They are covered below
for completeness and flagged as non-built where relevant.

---

## A. Remediated — dependencies in the built reactor

| # | Package | Was | Now | CVEs | Where fixed |
|---|---------|-----|-----|------|-------------|
| 1 | com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-databind | 2.18.2 | 2.18.8 | CVE-2026-54512, -54513, -54514, -54515 | parent property `jackson.databind` |
| 2 | com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-core | 2.18.2 | 2.18.8 | GHSA-72hv-8253-57qq | follows databind (transitive) |
| 3 | jackson-jaxrs-base / jackson-jaxrs-json-provider / jackson-datatype-jsr310 | 2.18.2 | 2.18.8 | same jackson family | `grouper-ws/grouper-ws/pom.xml` (hard-coded) |
| 4 | com.google.protobuf:protobuf-java | 4.26.1 | 4.28.2 | CVE-2024-7254 | parent `dependencyManagement` |
| 5 | com.nimbusds:nimbus-jose-jwt | 9.37 (and 9.25.6) | 9.37.4 | CVE-2023-52428, CVE-2025-53864 | parent `dependencyManagement` |
| 6 | io.netty:netty-* (codec, codec-http, common, handler, transport-native-epoll/kqueue) | 4.1.72.Final | 4.1.135.Final | CVE-2022-24823, -2024-29025, -2023-34462, -2024-47535, -2025-25193, -2025-58056/58057, -2025-67735, -2026-33870, -2026-41417, -2026-42580/42581/42583/42584/42585/42587, -2026-44249, -2026-45416/45536, -2026-50010/50020 | parent `dependencyManagement` (import `netty-bom` 4.1.135.Final) |
| 7 | ion-java | software.amazon.ion:ion-java 1.0.2 | com.amazon.ion:ion-java 1.10.5 | CVE-2024-21634 | exclude old artifact from `aws-java-sdk-core` + add new coordinate in `grouper/pom.xml` (managed in parent) |
| 8 | org.apache.james:apache-mime4j-core | 0.7.2 | 0.8.11 | CVE-2024-21742 | parent `dependencyManagement` |
| 9 | org.apache.neethi:neethi | 3.0.2 | 3.2.2 | CVE-2026-42402, -42403, -42404 | parent `dependencyManagement` |
| 10 | com.google.guava:guava | 31.1-jre | 32.1.3-jre | CVE-2023-2976, CVE-2020-8908 | parent `dependencyManagement` |
| 11 | com.google.oauth-client:google-oauth-client | 1.33.2 | 1.34.1 | CVE-2021-22573 | parent `dependencyManagement` |
| 12 | org.bouncycastle:bcprov-jdk15on + bcpkix-jdk15on | 1.52 | excluded → bcprov/bcpkix-jdk18on 1.84 | 17 CVEs incl. CVE-2016-1000338..352, CVE-2018-1000180, CVE-2020-15522/26939, CVE-2023-33201/33202, CVE-2024-29857/30171 | exclude from `box-java-sdk` and use jdk18on 1.84 in `grouper-box/pom.xml` |
| 13 | org.bitbucket.b_c:jose4j | 0.9.3 | 0.9.6 | CVE-2023-51775, CVE-2024-29371 | `grouper-box/pom.xml` (hard-coded) |
| 14 | org.apache.commons:commons-compress | 1.25.0 | 1.27.1 | CVE-2024-25710, CVE-2024-26308 | `grouper-installer/pom.xml` (hard-coded) |
| 15 | org.opensaml:opensaml | 2.6.4 | 2.6.5 | CVE-2015-1796 | parent property `opensaml.version` |

### Notes on the bouncycastle fix (#12)
`com.box:box-java-sdk:2.17.0` transitively pulls the **discontinued**
`bcprov-jdk15on` / `bcpkix-jdk15on` at 1.52. The `jdk15on` line has no release
past 1.70 and several of its CVEs are only fixed in the `jdk18on` line, so a
version bump alone cannot remediate it. The old artifacts are therefore
**excluded** from `box-java-sdk` and replaced with `bcprov-jdk18on` /
`bcpkix-jdk18on` 1.84 (the version the rest of the reactor already uses via the
parent). Same `org.bouncycastle` package, so it is a drop-in replacement.

### Notes on the ion-java fix (#7)
`com.amazonaws:aws-java-sdk-core:1.12.267` pulls
`software.amazon.ion:ion-java:1.0.2`. CVE-2024-21634 is only fixed under the
**newer coordinate** `com.amazon.ion:ion-java` (the old
`software.amazon.ion:ion-java` line stops at 1.5.1 — 1.10.5 does not exist under
it). Both publish the identical `com.amazon.ion` Java package, so the old
artifact is excluded and `com.amazon.ion:ion-java:1.10.5` is added.

---

## B. Remediated — non-built module

| # | Package | Was | Now | CVEs | Where |
|---|---------|-----|-----|------|-------|
| 16 | org.apache.activemq:activemq-broker, activemq-jaas (+ transitive activemq-client) | 5.19.2 | 5.19.7 | CVE-2026-33227, -34197, -39304, -40466, -41043, -41044, -42588, -45505, -49270 | `grouper-misc/grouperActivemq/pom.xml` (hard-coded). Module is **not** in the `make test` reactor, but the versions were still bumped. |

---

## C. Identified but NOT fixed — no fixed version exists (left as-is, by design)

Per the task instructions, these are called out explicitly rather than guessed at.

| Package | Version | CVE(s) | Why not fixed |
|---------|---------|--------|---------------|
| commons-lang:commons-lang | 2.6 | CVE-2025-48924 | No fixed release in the legacy `commons-lang` 2.x line. The advisory is only resolved in `org.apache.commons:commons-lang3` **3.18.0** — a different artifact. `commons-lang` 2.6 here is a transitive legacy dependency; the reactor already ships `commons-lang3` 3.20.0 (not vulnerable). No drop-in version fix available. |
| net.sf.json-lib:json-lib | 2.4 | CVE-2024-47855 | No fixed release published for `net.sf.json-lib:json-lib`. The library is effectively unmaintained; there is no newer non-vulnerable version to move to. |
| org.hibernate:hibernate-core | 5.6.10.Final | CVE-2026-0603 | No fixed release exists in the Hibernate 5.x line; remediation would require a major upgrade to Hibernate 6.x/7.x, which is an API-breaking change well outside a version bump. Flagged, not changed. |
| commons-httpclient:commons-httpclient | 3.1 | CVE-2012-5783 | No fixed release in the legacy `commons-httpclient` 3.x line. The advisory's "fixed 4.0" refers to the successor artifact `org.apache.httpcomponents:httpclient` (already present in the reactor at 4.5.13) — an API-incompatible replacement, not a version bump. Present directly in `grouper-installer` and transitively in `grouper-ws`. |

---

## D. Identified but NOT auto-applied — fix exists but is a breaking major upgrade

| Package | Version | CVE | Fixed in | Why not applied |
|---------|---------|-----|----------|-----------------|
| org.apache.axis2:axis2-transport-http | 1.6.4 | CVE-2012-5785 (hostname verification, MEDIUM) | 1.8.0 | `grouper-ws` hard-codes the whole Axis2 SOAP stack on 1.6.x (`axis2-kernel`, `axis2-adb`, `axis2-transport-local`, `axis2-transport-http`). Moving only `axis2-transport-http` to 1.8.0 mixes incompatible Axis2 majors, and moving the entire stack to 1.8.0 is an API-breaking upgrade that does not compile against the current `grouper-ws` code. Left on 1.6.4 to keep the build green; flagged here for a follow-up dedicated Axis2 upgrade. |

---

## E. Non-built modules — ancient transitive stacks (flagged, not remediated)

These modules are **excluded from the `make test` reactor** (commented out in
`grouper-parent/pom.xml` or not referenced as reactor modules), so their
dependencies are not on the built application's classpath. They are recorded for
completeness; remediating them requires major framework upgrades, not version
bumps:

- **`grouper-misc/grouper-shib`** — depends on `shibboleth-common`/`opensaml`
  2.6.x, which drags in a large set of end-of-life libraries flagged by the
  scanner: `spring-*` 2.5.6.SEC02 (CVE-2022-22965 "Spring4Shell", CVE-2016-1000027,
  and many more), `org.apache.santuario:xmlsec` 1.5.7/1.5.8 (CVE-2021-40690),
  `org.apache.velocity:velocity` 1.7 (CVE-2020-13936, no fix),
  `xalan:xalan` 2.7.1 (CVE-2022-34169), `org.owasp.esapi:esapi` 2.0.1,
  `edu.vt.middleware:vt-ldap` 3.3.4, `ca.juliusdavies:not-yet-commons-ssl` 0.3.9,
  and `bcprov-jdk15on` 1.51. There is no version-bump remediation; it requires
  migrating off the OpenSAML 2 / Shibboleth 1.x stack.
- **`grouper-misc/grouper-ext-auth`** — `org.pac4j:pac4j-*` 5.7.2
  (CVE-2026-40458, fixed 5.7.10) and `spring-core` 5.3.24 (CVE-2025-41249,
  fixed 6.2.11 — a major upgrade). Not in the build.
- **`grouper-misc/test-plugin`** and the `grouper/src/test/.../testImplementation`
  sample — `commons-lang3` 3.12.0 (CVE-2025-48924, fixed 3.18.0). Standalone
  sample POMs, not in the build; the reactor itself uses 3.20.0.
- **`grouper-ws/grouper-ws-java-generated-client`** — `bcpkix-jdk15on` 1.70
  (CVE-2025-8916 etc.). Non-built client module.
</content>
</invoke>

text: Let me confirm the final set of changes.

text:
Remediation is complete and the build passes. Here's a summary.

## What I did

I resolved the full 13-module reactor's dependency tree (`mvn dependency:tree`) and scanned both the POMs and the resolved artifacts with `trivy`, then mapped every finding to the exact place it enters (hard-coded version, parent property, or transitive) so each fix landed correctly. `make test` passes, and a re-run of the tree confirms the intended versions now resolve.

## Fixed (built reactor)
- **jackson-databind/-core + 3 jaxrs/datatype artifacts** 2.18.2 → **2.18.8** (parent property + grouper-ws)
- **protobuf-java** 4.26.1 → **4.28.2**, **nimbus-jose-jwt** 9.37 → **9.37.4**, **apache-mime4j-core** 0.7.2 → **0.8.11**, **neethi** 3.0.2 → **3.2.2**, **guava** 31.1 → **32.1.3-jre**, **google-oauth-client** 1.33.2 → **1.34.1** (parent `dependencyManagement`)
- **netty** 4.1.72 → **4.1.135.Final** via imported `netty-bom` (clears ~20 CVEs across codec-http/handler/common/transports)
- **ion-java**: excluded EOL `software.amazon.ion:ion-java` 1.0.2, added `com.amazon.ion:ion-java` **1.10.5** (fix only exists under the new coordinate)
- **bcprov/bcpkix-jdk15on** 1.52: excluded from `box-java-sdk`, replaced with **jdk18on 1.84** (the discontinued jdk15on line can't be bumped out of its CVEs)
- **jose4j** 0.9.3 → **0.9.6**, **commons-compress** 1.25.0 → **1.27.1**, **opensaml** 2.6.4 → **2.6.5**
- **activemq** 5.19.2 → **5.19.7** (non-built `grouperActivemq`, bumped anyway)

## Explicitly NOT fixed — no version fix exists (documented, not guessed)
- **commons-lang 2.6** (CVE-2025-48924 — only fixed in the different `commons-lang3` artifact)
- **json-lib 2.4** (CVE-2024-47855 — unmaintained, no fixed release)
- **hibernate-core 5.6.10** (CVE-2026-0603 — no 5.x fix; needs Hibernate 6 major upgrade)
- **commons-httpclient 3.1** (CVE-2012-5783 — "fix" is the successor httpclient 4.x artifact, already present)

## Fix exists but is a breaking major upgrade (flagged)
- **axis2-transport-http 1.6.
